summary: Backend-only validation completed for env-driven callback architecture. All targeted regression probes passed, and invalid INDODAX_CALLBACK_PATH correctly fails fast to protect stable internal routing.
action_items: Keep INDODAX_CALLBACK_PATH stability guard (must remain /indodax/callback) as a protected regression gate.
Keep yarn render:nginx in deployment pipeline to prevent manual nginx drift from env contract.
Preserve private_api_v2_mapping_probe and app/http lifecycle probes as required checks before release.
critical_code_review_comments: No blocking code-level regression found in this scope: env callback derivation, stable internal callback route enforcement, nginx rendering wiring, and Indodax v2 mapping are consistent.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data created. Isolated temporary DATA_DIR paths used during probes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed and passed: yarn lint, yarn build, yarn render:nginx, private_api_v2_mapping_probe, http_servers_probe, nginx_renderer_probe, app_lifecycle_servers_probe. Explicit negative test also confirmed: INDODAX_CALLBACK_PATH=/hooks/indodax fails fast in both app boot path and render:nginx with message 'must remain /indodax/callback to keep internal route stable'. Note: some repository probes use MOCKED exchange/telegram harnesses by design.
rca of the issue: No defect reproduced in requested scope. Prior risk area (callback path drift) is mitigated by readStablePath validation in both src/config/env.ts and scripts/render-nginx-conf.mjs, which blocks startup/render when path deviates from /indodax/callback.
